pr.title: Splitting and matching of parts of sysdolphin via DTK
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Used DTK to split out a large portion of sysdolphin/baselib into configured object-file ranges and to improve matching/naming metadata. The PR added many GALE01 split entries for baselib files such as tobj, state, tev, mobj, aobj, lobj, cobj, fobj, pobj, jobj, spline, util, objalloc, robj, id, wobj, list, object, random, texp, and texpdag. It also heavily updated symbols.txt, replacing many address-based fn_ names with known runtime/GX/OS/MTX/PSVEC names and marking some data/scope attributes. Small source edits in fobj.c, mobj.c, and lobj.c adjusted includes or code to resolve build/matching issues. The PR body explicitly framed this as DTK groundwork and a reference for others learning DTK.
what_changed: Added DTK split ranges in config/GALE01/splits.txt for numerous sysdolphin/baselib compilation units, including text-only files and files with .data, .bss, .sdata, .sbss, and .sdata2 ranges.

Converted many anonymous fn_ symbols in config/GALE01/symbols.txt into known SDK/runtime names, including __setjmp, longjmp, __cvt_fp2unsigned, __div2i, __mod2i, PPCMtdec, __DBIsExceptionMarked, GXCPInterruptHandler, GXInitFifoPtrs, GXInitFifoLimits, many GXSet/GXInit/GXLoad functions, PSMTX/PSVEC matrix-vector symbols, OSExceptionInit, and related OS/GX internals.
Updated symbol metadata such as scope and data type in symbols.txt; one reviewed example changed lbl_804D5C18 into a local string symbol @32 with data:string.
Changed configure.py to support the new DTK/nonmatching setup, though the provided excerpt does not show the exact logic.
Made small edits in fobj.c, mobj.c, and lobj.c. Review discussion confirms fobj.c removed an unused forward.h include and changed the fobj.h include path to resolve a build error.
smart_moves: Split sysdolphin/baselib at object-file granularity with explicit section ranges, creating a reusable map for later matching work instead of treating the region as one monolithic blob.
Used DTK not only to match current pieces but also to set up NonMatching configurations for files that still needed work, lowering the barrier for follow-up contributors.
Improved symbols from address placeholders to canonical SDK-style names where evidence was available, making future analysis and cross-referencing much more searchable.
Kept config-heavy progress separate from broad source rewrites; only minimal C source changes were made where needed for build or matching friction.
Exposed review-relevant DTK behavior, such as whether string data annotations affect asm dump output, which is useful for future symbol metadata decisions.
decomp_lessons: For DTK-driven decomp, landing accurate splits.txt ranges can be valuable infrastructure even before every file is fully matched.

When adding many split entries, include all relevant sections per file, not just .text, because baselib files may own .data, .bss, .sdata, .sbss, and .sdata2 ranges needed for correct object reconstruction.
Replacing fn_ADDRESS placeholders with known SDK names is high leverage for decomp work, but review may ask whether the naming was automated or manually verified.

String/data annotations in symbols.txt can affect generated assembly representation; reviewers specifically asked whether data:string changes the asm dump to asciz, so future PRs should verify and document such effects.

Include cleanup should preserve project tooling assumptions: clang-format is configured to sort includes matching the current file above others, and the Make toolchain/editor patterns should work with the standard include style.
If a header path is changed to fix a build error, explain the build failure and remove truly unused includes rather than keeping forward declarations by habit.
naming_conventions: Known SDK/runtime symbols use their canonical leading-underscore forms where applicable, e.g. __setjmp, __div2i, __mod2i, __GXSetDirtyState, __OSDBIntegrator.
GX functions are named with Nintendo SDK-style prefixes such as GXSet*, GXInit*, GXLoad*, __GX*, and GXPoke*.
Matrix and vector functions use PSMTX*, C_MTX*, and PSVEC* naming families.
PowerPC helper symbols use PPC* capitalization, e.g. PPCMtdec, PPCMfhid0, PPCMfl2cr.
Unknown functions remain address-derived fn_803xxxxx placeholders rather than receiving speculative names.
Local data/string symbols may use DTK-generated local labels such as @32 with scope:local and data:string metadata.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use config/GALE01/splits.txt to assign exact start/end addresses for each sysdolphin/baselib source file and section, enabling DTK to produce object-level comparisons.
Set up NonMatching configurations for files that are split but not yet fully matched, allowing incremental progress without forcing immediate full matches.
Leverage DTK or symbol-import automation for bulk known-SDK symbol naming, but be ready to identify which names were automated versus manual.
Use symbols.txt metadata such as type:function, type:object, size, scope:global/local, and data:string to influence dumps and matching artifacts.
Resolve build mismatches with minimal include/source adjustments; the fobj.c review notes removing forward.h when unused and changing fobj.h path to fix a build error.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.comment: Questioned the include pattern change, noting that the common editor pattern and Make toolchain should recognize the standard format and that clang-format sorts includes matching the current file above others.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.comment: Explained that the fobj.h path was changed to resolve a build error and forward.h was removed because it was not needed.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.comment: Asked whether marking data as a string changes it to asciz in the asm dump.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.comment: Asked whether the large symbol renaming pass was automated.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.comment: Stated that at least one naming/data effect occurred automatically because of the code rather than manual work, and also answered 'I did not' to one review question; the excerpt does not provide enough context to map every reply unambiguously.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that build on the sysdolphin/baselib DTK splits added in PR1071.
Search for follow-up changes to configure.py related to DTK NonMatching configuration.
Check whether symbols marked data:string in symbols.txt generated asciz in asm dumps after this PR.
Audit remaining fn_8033xxxx GX/OS symbols after PR1071 for additional SDK name imports.
Review later fobj.c include/header changes to see whether the include path workaround remained necessary.
